skill.name: terraform-specialist
skill.description: Provides expert guidance for Terraform and OpenTofu infrastructure design, secure state management, module architecture, policy checks, GitOps workflows, and CI/CD automation.
skill.summary: Guide Terraform and OpenTofu infrastructure design, governance, automation, and state operations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 20 high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Terraform documentation terms, compliance text, and words containing algorithm-like substrings. The skill is a single Markdown instruction file with no scripts, network access, external commands, credential access, or prompt-injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Terms
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm detections occur in Terraform and OpenTofu prose about module design, environments, lifecycle management, and maintenance. No evidence found of instructions to use weak algorithms such as DES, MD5, or SHA1.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are descriptive IaC guidance, not code or cryptographic configuration. The surrounding context recommends encryption and secure backends rather than weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: C2 Keyword Detection
low_findings.description: The C2 keyword detection is caused by the compliance term SOC2. No evidence found of command-and-control behavior, network endpoints, callbacks, or remote instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 113 lists compliance standards and contains SOC2 in a governance context. There is no executable content or networking behavior in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Terms
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance detections occur in safe infrastructure topics such as protecting state files, multi-cloud patterns, and hybrid deployments. No evidence found of host enumeration commands, secret discovery, or local system probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are section headings and defensive state-management guidance. They do not contain commands or instructions to inspect the user's machine.

content.value_statement: Infrastructure teams need repeatable Terraform and OpenTofu patterns that protect state, reduce drift, and support governance. This skill guides module design, automation, policy checks, and operational workflows.
actual_capabilities: Designs Terraform and OpenTofu module structures for reusable infrastructure.
Recommends remote state backends, locking, encryption, and workspace strategies.
Plans CI/CD workflows with plan review, policy gates, testing, and approvals.
Guides multi-cloud and hybrid infrastructure patterns across providers.
Supports migration planning from Terraform to OpenTofu.
Helps troubleshoot drift, failed applies, state issues, and module upgrades.
limitations: Does not execute Terraform plans or apply infrastructure changes.
Requires the user to provide provider, backend, and environment details.
Cannot verify live cloud account state without user-supplied context.
Does not replace security review for production infrastructure changes.

use_cases.title: Design reusable platform modules
use_cases.description: Create module boundaries, inputs, outputs, tests, and versioning rules for shared infrastructure components.

use_cases.title: Harden state and deployment workflows
use_cases.description: Plan remote state, locking, encryption, approval gates, and recovery procedures for team-based Terraform use.

use_cases.title: Review infrastructure governance
use_cases.description: Map policy as code, compliance checks, drift detection, and cost controls into an IaC delivery process.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the right structure
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a new Terraform repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me structure a Terraform repository for three environments. Include folders, state strategy, module boundaries, and review steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design a secure module
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a reusable Terraform module for a web service. Include inputs, outputs, validation, testing, tagging, and upgrade guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Plan CI/CD governance
prompt_templates.scenario: Team adding automation and policy checks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Terraform CI/CD workflow with plan review, policy as code, security scanning, approval gates, and rollback procedures.

prompt_templates.title: Architect enterprise IaC operations
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess our multi-cloud Terraform operating model. Recommend module strategy, state isolation, provider governance, drift detection, and OpenTofu migration steps.

output_examples.input: Design a remote state approach for three product teams.
output_examples.output: A recommended backend layout with separate state per environment, locking requirements, encryption controls, access boundaries, backup policy, and recovery steps.

output_examples.input: Review our Terraform delivery workflow for production.
output: Plan review happens before any apply.
Policy checks block unsafe resource changes.
Rollback and state recovery steps are documented.

output_examples.input: Help migrate modules from Terraform to OpenTofu.
output_examples.output: A migration checklist covering provider compatibility, state backup, version constraints, test environments, module validation, and rollout sequencing.

best_practices: Provide cloud provider, environment, compliance, and state backend details before asking for architecture guidance.
Review generated plans and policy results with the team before applying infrastructure changes.
Keep secrets out of state when possible and protect state with encryption, locking, backups, and least privilege.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for one shared state file across unrelated teams or environments.
Do not bypass plan review, policy checks, or approval gates for production changes.
Do not treat generated Terraform guidance as a substitute for live validation and security review.

faq.question: Can this skill write Terraform modules?
faq.answer: It can guide module design, inputs, outputs, tests, and documentation. Users must validate generated code in their own environment.

faq.question: Does it support OpenTofu?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes OpenTofu migration and compatibility guidance.

faq.question: Can it run terraform apply?
faq.answer: No. It provides planning and review guidance but does not execute infrastructure commands.

faq.question: Is it useful for multi-cloud infrastructure?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers provider abstraction, cross-provider dependencies, hybrid deployments, and multi-cloud module patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with compliance?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest policy as code, audit trails, access controls, and compliance-oriented review workflows.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide cloud providers, environments, state backend choices, security constraints, team workflow, and deployment goals.
